team: Andrea Cutroni, Rania Chihaoui, Symon Kipekemi
description: A flood-resilient floating urban model for Jakarta — housing, public program and a stadium organized by water rather than against it.
body: Rather than resisting flooding, the project accepts water as a permanent condition and uses it as an organizing element. A ~30,000 m² floating complex in Penjaringan merges residential housing, a stadium and a commercial buffer zone into a single resilience hub, all built on a standardized 5 × 5 m modular grid for construction efficiency, adaptability and scalability. Pontoons sized through load analysis and Archimedes' principle sit roughly 1.5 m deep in the water.

A Grasshopper-based parametric workflow drove solar orientation, wind and energy modeling, and stadium sightlines were verified with the C-value method. The optimized massing cuts solar radiation by over 30% compared to a simple box, while ~780,000 kWh of annual on-site photovoltaic production offsets nearly 40% of the total cooling load.
